segments.integrated_caption: Amanda Davies, radiant in a strapless black gown with subtle sparkles and a diamond necklace, stands at the golden podium of the Dubai Globe Soccer Awards, smiling as she introduces the 'Best Women's Club' category, her clear voice announcing the "finalists for the Dubai Globe Soccer Awards Best Women's Club are...". The stage backdrop pulses with dynamic orange and yellow geometric light patterns as the scene transitions to a golden animated graphic announcing the 'BEST WOMEN'S CLUB' award. The logos of Barcelona, Chelsea, and Arsenal appear sequentially, each accompanied by the 'KOORA BREAK' sponsor logo, as Amanda's voice distinctly names "FC Barcelona," "Chelsea," and "Arsenal." The formally dressed audience, seated in white armchairs, including some in traditional white keffiyehs, is shown clapping and watching attentively, their applause audible. Tom Urquhart, in a black tuxedo with a bow tie, then takes the podium, speaking into a lapel microphone with expressive hand gestures, his formal tone declaring, "The best women's club at the Dubai Globe Soccer Awards is... FC Barcelona!" The audience erupts in enthusiastic applause and cheers. A montage of Barcelona women's football highlights follows, featuring a goal celebration, a player in a blue and red jersey scoring, and dynamic gameplay, with text overlays announcing '8TH LIGA F TITLE' and '2ND UEFA WOMEN'S CHAMPIONS LEAGUE TITLE'. A male commentator's voice details their dominance: "It's been another dominant year for FC Barcelona. They landed their eighth Liga F title and a counter, winning 28 of 30 games, scoring 118 goals, conceding just 10 times. This was their fourth title in a row, having exemplified their domestic dominance, they have a score to settle." The montage concludes with a graphic stating 'WON 28 FROM 30 LEAGUE GAMES SCORED 118 GOALS', underscoring Barcelona's exceptional season amidst the celebratory and high-energy atmosphere.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with dynamic highlights of FC Barcelona's women's team, set to triumphant, upbeat music, as a male voiceover details their dominance, stating they "WON 28 FROM 30 LEAGUE GAMES" and "SCORED 118 GOALS," and highlighting their "Champions League" victory with a "3-2 victory" against Wolfsburg, securing the trophy for a "second time in three seasons." Celebratory scenes of the team lifting the UEFA Women's Champions League trophy amidst confetti and jubilant fans are shown, accompanied by crowd cheering and applause. segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of the Presenter in Dark Suit, a man with grey, curly hair and glasses, formally dressed in a dark suit, white shirt, black bow tie, and white pocket square. He stands authoritatively at the Golden Podium, equipped with two microphones and a bottle of water, against a dark backdrop illuminated by dynamic, pulsing orange and yellow geometric light patterns, prominently displaying 'BARCELONA' in white text. He begins his speech with a clear, formal voice, greeting "Good evening everyone." He announces, "It is a pleasure for me to represent FC Barcelona in the occasion of receiving the Best Women's Team of the Year award at the Global Soccer Awards." He emphasizes that "This award [is] the result of many people's work and the club's commitment to women's football." Occasionally gesturing with his hands and glancing at his notes, he continues, "On behalf of your player Aitana Bonmatí, I also want to thank Globe Soccer Awards for the Best Player award." He explains that "Aitana is currently in Madrid, focusing on the Super Cup final that they play tomorrow and we hope will be our first trophy this season." The camera remains steady, capturing his composed expressions as he concludes, "Thank you again for this award. We are immensely honored. Thank you very much." His speech is met with applause, and a female voice is heard saying "Congratulations," reinforcing the celebratory atmosphere of the Dubai Globe Soccer Awards ceremony.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a close-up of Cristiano Ronaldo, dressed in a dark blue textured suit and black turtleneck, smiling and clapping alongside other formally dressed attendees, including a man in a white keffiyeh. The scene then transitions to a wide shot of the Dubai Globe Soccer Awards stage, featuring a dynamic orange and yellow geometric light design on the backdrop displaying 'NAKHEEL' and 'DUBAI SPORTS COUNCIL'. The Presenter in Dark Suit, wearing a black tuxedo, stands at the Golden Podium, gesturing expressively as he thanks the audience and congratulates 'all the team at FC Barcelona' for the 'Best Women's Club' award. He then announces a moment to 'honor a couple of careers,' describing it as a 'nice tradition' to say 'thank you to some of the greats of the game' for their 'sheer genius.' Amanda Davies, in a strapless black gown and diamond necklace, joins him at the podium, her voice clear as she announces that the 'first career award has been generously sponsored by Serie A' and goes to 'former England and Chelsea captain, John Terry.' The camera pans across the applauding audience, showing attendees in white armchairs, some filming on their phones. A man in a black suit, identified as the Winner in Black Suit (John Terry), rises from his seat and walks down the aisle towards the stage, where he is greeted by the Recipient in Green Thobe, who presents him with a golden trophy. segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of John Terry, dressed in a dark velvet tuxedo with a black bow tie, receiving the 'John Terry Player Career Award' from a man in a green thobe and white keffiyeh, while another man in a white thobe stands beside them. Dynamic orange and yellow geometric light patterns illuminate the stage, and the backdrop displays 'NAKHEEL PRESENTS DUBAI GLOBESOCCER AWARDS' along with sponsor logos, accompanied by upbeat background music. A female voice announces, "Congratulations to John, let's see if we can get a few words. Come on over," followed by a friendly "Hello." The camera then pulls back to a wide shot revealing the full stage, where Terry walks to a golden circular podium on the right side, while a woman in a black gown stands at a similar podium on the left. Terry begins his acceptance speech, standing at the podium with a microphone, a bottle of water, and his notes, greeting the audience with "Good evening everyone." He expresses his honor in winning the award, thanking the Dubai Sports Council and Globe Soccer Awards for hosting. He emphasizes that individual awards are fantastic but impossible without teammates and managers, stating his fortune in playing with great managers and the best players. The camera alternates between close-ups of Terry speaking with a clear, appreciative voice and wide shots of the audience, who are seated in white armchairs, clapping and observing attentively. The audience includes formally dressed attendees, some in traditional white keffiyehs, and others in suits, with Erling Haaland visible in a blue tuxedo adjusting his cufflinks. Terry dedicates his award to Gianluca Vialli, who gave him his debut and sadly lost his life last year, prompting enthusiastic applause from the audience. He continues, "Luca was a manager that believed in me and gave me my first opportunity," as the stage backdrop features large screens displaying 'JOHN TERRY' in bold white letters, flanked by animated light streaks, maintaining the celebratory and formal atmosphere.

segments.integrated_caption: John Terry, distinguished in a dark velvet tuxedo and black bow tie, stands at a golden circular podium on a grand stage, holding a golden globe-shaped trophy in his left hand and a small black device in his right. With his name 'JOHN TERRY' boldly displayed on the dynamic orange and yellow geometric light patterns of the backdrop, he speaks into the microphone, expressing gratitude for his time at Chelsea, dedicating his award to Luca Vialli with an emotional, "Luca, this one's for you. I love you and thank you." The audience, including men in traditional keffiyehs and women in elegant gowns, responds with applause. Amanda Davies, in a sparkling strapless black gown and diamond necklace, approaches from the left podium, holding a blue folder. She calls out to John Terry, then asks him, "Just before you go. If there was one thing that Luca Vialli taught you, told you, that you carry with you, what would it be?" John Terry, smiling, recounts Vialli's advice from the showers: "Don't do what I do, do what I say," emphasizing its lasting impact. Amanda then playfully asks, "I'm just checking, have you got your kit on underneath there?" to which John Terry laughs and confirms, "I have actually, yeah." The camera briefly shows a wide shot of the Dubai Globe Soccer Awards stage, with 'NAKHEEL PRESENTS DUBAI GLOBESOCCER AWARDS' branding, and two other figures in traditional thobes near the center. Returning to the close-up, Amanda shifts to a more serious tone, inquiring about Chelsea's current performance: "Chelsea at the moment, it's not been the best run for them... If you were in that dressing room, what would your message be?" John Terry thoughtfully responds, reflecting on football's cyclical nature, mentioning past dominance by Manchester United and Chelsea, as the vibrant stage lighting continues to pulse, reinforcing the celebratory atmosphere of the 'PLAYER CAREER AWARD' presentation.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of John Terry, dressed in a dark velvet tuxedo with a black bow tie, speaking at a golden circular podium during the Dubai Globe Soccer Awards. He holds a golden globe-shaped trophy in his left hand and a a small black device in his right, with a bottle of water and notes beside him. He reflects on Manchester City's dominance, stating it's "hard to catch them" as they are "the best team" with "great individual players" and their consistent performance "year after year is incredible." He contrasts this with Chelsea, admitting they are "a little bit off it at the moment" but expresses confidence in their manager, Pochettino, whom he's seen "work on a daily basis," and their "young squad of players," though acknowledging they are "a long way off" the likes of Man City and Liverpool. The backdrop features dynamic orange and yellow geometric light patterns and the name 'JOHN TERRY' in bold white letters. The camera then pulls back to reveal a wider stage view, showing Amanda Davies in a strapless black gown with a diamond necklace, standing at a second identical golden podium to the left, engaging in conversation with John Terry, congratulating him and telling him to "enjoy this moment," to which he responds with "Thank you." The stage is flanked by two men in traditional white thobes and keffiyehs, standing near the center. The backdrop displays the 'NAKHEEL PRESENTS DUBAI GLOBESOCCER AWARDS' logo with sponsor logos including Dubai Sports Council and Nakheel. The audience, seated in white armchairs, includes men in traditional white keffiyehs and women in elegant gowns, some capturing the event on their phones. The camera then focuses on a presenter in a dark suit with grey, curly hair, glasses, a white shirt, black bow tie, and white pocket square, standing at the golden podium, speaking into microphones and gesturing with his hands. He reiterates "congratulations to John, well deserved," before introducing the "next of our awards," paying tribute to "yet another star who's secured his place in the history books of the game," noting the award is "brought to you by SilverSun." The podium features the 'NAKHEEL PRESENTS DUBAI GLOBESOCCER AWARDS' logo, and a bottle of water is beside him. The scene transitions back to Amanda Davies, who smiles and speaks into the microphone, holding a blue folder with the event's logo, beginning to announce, "Ladies and gentlemen, our next career award winner is none..." The stage lighting remains dynamic with pulsing orange and yellow geometric patterns, creating a celebratory atmosphere.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with Amanda Davies, elegantly dressed in a strapless black gown with subtle sparkles and a diamond necklace, standing at the golden podium of the Nakheel Dubai Globe Soccer Awards. Her clear voice announces "World Cup winning coach, Lionel Scaloni," as the stage is illuminated by pulsing orange and yellow geometric light patterns. The scene cuts to the audience, where formally dressed attendees, including men in traditional white keffiyehs and women in gowns, are seated in white armchairs, some capturing the event on their phones, and applauding loudly. Upbeat, celebratory orchestral music begins as the narrative shifts to a dynamic montage celebrating Lionel Scaloni’s achievements, narrated by a male voice detailing his journey from taking over a struggling Argentina team in 2018 to helping them find a new identity. The narration highlights the team's flourishing success, including the 2021 Copa America victory that ended a 28-year drought, and their "date with destiny in Qatar" culminating in the 2022 FIFA World Cup win, making Argentina world champions for a third time and catapulting Scaloni to greatness. On-screen text further emphasizes his status as the fourth youngest coach to win the World Cup, accompanied by footage of Argentina players celebrating, Scaloni on the field, and the team disembarking from an airplane with medals. The video culminates with Scaloni walking down the red carpet pathway toward the stage, where he is presented with the golden Coach Career Award by two men in traditional attire—one in a green thobe and the other in a white thobe—against a backdrop of glowing lights and the event’s branding, with the celebratory music continuing as the audience applauds his reception of the trophy.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with an upbeat, celebratory orchestral score as Lionel Scaloni, dressed in a black suit, receives the golden globe-shaped Coach Career Award from two men in traditional Emirati attire. He smiles, waves to the audience, and walks towards the center stage of the Dubai Globe Soccer Awards, where a large screen displays his name and event branding. A female voice announces, "So, congratulations. The mic is yours," as Scaloni approaches a golden circular podium. He begins his acceptance speech, first apologizing in English, "Hello, sorry for my English is very bad. I speak Spanish," before switching to Spanish. Gesturing with his hands and occasionally glancing at notes beside a bottle of water, he thanks for the award, dedicating it to his country and people, stating, "Honestly, what we've achieved has been like a movie, a film." He then dedicates it to "all Argentinians" and his technical staff, specifically naming Pablo Aimar, Walter Samuel, Roberto Ayala, Matías Manna, Martín Tocalli, Luis Martín, and Rodrigo Barrios, acknowledging their help in achieving "this incredible..." The audience, seated in white armchairs, watches attentively, some filming, while the stage backdrop pulses with orange and yellow geometric lights. A brief cutaway shows Cristiano Ronaldo in the audience, appearing emotional as he wipes his face, before returning to Scaloni, who continues his composed and reflective speech, maintaining eye contact, amidst the grand, celebratory atmosphere.

Thank you, thank you." Behind him, a large screen displays his name against pulsing orange and yellow geometric light patterns. As he speaks, he gestures subtly and glances at notes, with a bottle of water beside him. The camera then cuts to a close-up of Cristiano Ronaldo in the audience, wearing a dark blue textured suit over a black turtleneck, appearing emotional as he wipes his face with his hand, a diamond earring and large ring visible. A wide shot reveals the formally dressed audience, seated in white armchairs, some capturing the event on their phones, erupting in applause after Scaloni's speech. The scene shifts to a grand stage with three golden podiums, where two hosts, Tom Urquhart in a black tuxedo and Amanda Davies in a strapless black gown, flank a central figure in a green thobe and white keffiyeh, who presents the golden, globe-shaped Coach Career Award to Lionel Scaloni. The stage backdrop features the 'NAKHEEL PRESENTS DUBAI GLOBESOCCER AWARDS' logo, glowing geometric patterns, and sponsor logos. Tom Urquhart then takes the podium, speaking into a lapel microphone with expressive gestures, thanking the audience for their patience and those tuning in worldwide, before introducing the next award for "the finest club in the world," emphasizing the never-ending quest for balance and how "the good get separated from the great." Amanda Davies, holding a blue folder, then steps forward, lightheartedly asking if Scaloni persuaded Messi to stay for 2026, before engaging in conversation with Tom and beginning to announce the nominees for "the best men's..." all set against a backdrop of dramatic warm orange and yellow lighting and celebratory orchestral background music, as the audience continues to applaud.

segments.integrated_caption: The Nakheel Presents Dubai Globe Soccer Awards 2023 unfolds with a vibrant stage backdrop of pulsing orange and yellow geometric light patterns, set to the continuous sound of crowd applause. Tom Urquhart, in a black tuxedo with a bow tie, stands at the golden podium, speaking into a lapel microphone and gesturing, his clear male voice announcing, "The club, who also lifted the FA Cup for the seventh time in their history." The camera then cuts to a man in a black suit holding a large, ornate silver trophy with blue and white ribbons, walking confidently across the stage, soon joined by another man in a black suit with a similar trophy, standing side by side. Amanda Davies, elegantly dressed in a strapless black gown with a diamond necklace, appears at the golden podium, holding a blue folder with the event's logo, engaging with Tom. Her clear female voice declares, "The undefeated winners of the UEFA Champions League!" as the stage backdrop dynamically displays 'BEST MEN'S CLUB' alongside Dubai Sports Council and Nakheel logos. Tom Urquhart continues, "Which of course allowed them to go on to win the UEFA Super Cup for the very first time!" The two men with trophies are then joined by a third, forming a line of three, their synchronized movements and the gleaming trophies highlighted under the stage lights. Amanda Davies concludes, her voice announcing, "And the team who landed the FIFA Club World Cup on their tournament debut!" as a man in a black suit walks across the stage with a trophy, emphasizing the celebratory atmosphere of the awards ceremony.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens at the Nakheel Dubai Globe Soccer Awards with three men in black suits, holding ornate silver trophies adorned with blue and white ribbons, walking confidently onto a stage beside a host in a black tuxedo at a golden podium. The stage backdrop dynamically displays 'BEST MEN'S CLUB' with glowing orange and yellow geometric patterns. The host, speaking into a microphone, builds suspense, asking "So, any ideas?" before dramatically announcing, "Well, the Best Men's Club are none other than Manchester City!" This declaration is met with enthusiastic applause and cheering from the formally dressed audience, who are seen capturing the moment on their phones. The scene then transitions to a dynamic football highlight montage, accompanied by an upbeat, celebratory soundtrack and a narrator's voice. The montage begins with a player in a red and white striped jersey scoring, overlaid with text proclaiming 'FIRST ENGLISH SIDE TO WIN 5 MAJOR TROPHIES IN A CALENDAR YEAR'. Further footage shows a Manchester City player in a light blue jersey scoring, with text highlighting their 'WON 28 OF 38 PREMIER LEAGUE GAMES WITH A 73% WIN RATIO'. The narrator details their "global success," stating, "Victory over Inter Milan, City landed the UEFA Champions League title for the very first time," calling it "a culmination of years of hard work." The montage continues with the team celebrating on a confetti-strewn podium, then lifting the UEFA Champions League trophy, as text confirms they were 'UNBEATEN IN WINNING THEIR FIRST CHAMPIONS LEAGUE & FIFA CLUB WORLD CUP TITLES'. The narrator concludes by explaining that "Victory over Fluminense, the final piece of the puzzle was placed. As City won the FIFA Club World Cup," making them "the first English club to simultaneously hold the Premier League, FA Cup, UEFA Champions League, UEFA Super Cup, and the FIFA..." before the audio fades.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a vibrant montage celebrating Manchester City's recent triumphs, showing players on a podium lifting the UEFA Champions League trophy amidst a shower of confetti and fireworks in a packed stadium, as a male voiceover declares their 'sporting immortality' after securing the Club World Cup. This transitions to a stunning nighttime aerial view of the Atlantis The Palm Hotel, illuminated in blue and pink, with a dynamic graphic overlay announcing 'BEST MEN'S CLUB MANCHESTER CITY' over the iconic structure, accompanied by upbeat, celebratory background music. The scene then shifts to the grand interior of the Dubai Globe Soccer Awards venue, where a female host, identified as Amanda Davies, enthusiastically announces, 'What a season, what a trophy haul! Manchester City deservedly named Best Men's Club.' Formally dressed men, including players and officials in tuxedos, are seen walking through the applauding audience towards a golden-lit stage. Amanda Davies continues, inviting 'the rest of the team to come up and join us on stage,' specifically calling out 'Kevin' to 'come on over' and 'get your hands on another trophy.' On stage, a Manchester City player in a black suit, presumably Kevin, receives a large, golden, globe-shaped trophy from a dignitary in traditional Emirati attire, while Amanda Davies presents the award. The on-screen text confirms 'Manchester City Best Men's Club of the Year,' with the 'NAKHEEL PRESENTS DUBAI GLOBESOCCER AWARDS' logo and Manchester City's crest prominently displayed on the stage backdrop, as the audience continues to applaud and capture the moment on their phones.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a wide shot of the grand stage at the Nakheel Presents Dubai Globe Soccer Awards, where formally dressed individuals, including footballers and dignitaries, stand holding trophies under golden light patterns highlighting the 'BEST MEN'S CLUB' award and Manchester City's crest. A female voice, likely the host, encourages, "Come on, come on in, round for the team pick, everybody," followed by "Huddle in, huddle in." The audience, dressed in formal attire and traditional keffiyehs, applauds loudly as a male announcer exclaims, "So let's hear it one more time, give them a round of applause, your team at the end, Manchester City!" This is accompanied by celebratory music and continued applause. The camera then shows close-ups of clapping audience members before returning to the stage, where Amanda Davies, in a black strapless gown and diamond necklace, is seen holding a microphone and interviewing Rodrigo, who is in a black suit and holds a golden globe-shaped trophy. Behind him, Erling Haaland is visible in a striking blue tuxedo. Amanda Davies is heard asking, "So Kevin, we touched on it a little bit earlier, but this was the big one, wasn't it, for Manchester City, getting your hands on on the Champions League at last. What does that mean to you personally?" Rodrigo, seen speaking into the microphone, responds in a calm, reflective voice, explaining, "Um, well, it's the one that I missed with this team, you know. Um, I've been here for nine years and it's been a lot of uh, heartache, um, going through the Champions League, but you know, last year was uh, amazing and yeah, these guys helped me win this trophy, so I'm very grateful to everybody from the club for for the guys and..." During his speech, the camera zooms in on the UEFA Champions League trophy and later the Premier League trophy, both held by recipients in black suits, showcasing their silver surfaces and blue ribbons, all bathed in the warm, golden, celebratory lighting of the event.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a close-up of Amanda Davies, elegant in a strapless black gown with a diamond necklace, holding a microphone as she interviews Rodrigo, who is dressed in a black double-breasted suit and holds a golden globe-shaped trophy. Rodrigo's composed voice is heard reflecting on his team's performance, stating, "at least I got one," and later expressing confidence, "these guys are amazing... this team is ready to go again." Behind them, Erling Haaland, striking in a blue tuxedo, smiles. Amanda, addressing Rodrigo as "Gonn," playfully asks about his hair, to which he humorously replies, "Just don't go to the barber," eliciting laughter from Amanda and others. A brief cutaway shows a large, ornate silver Premier League trophy, adorned with blue and white ribbons, held by a man in a black suit. Returning to the interview, Amanda's clear voice congratulates "Manchester City and Kevin De Bruyne," as the audience, including men in traditional white keffiyehs, applauds enthusiastically. segments.integrated_caption: Against a backdrop of pulsing orange and yellow geometric light patterns, Tom Urquhart, a man in a black tuxedo with a bow tie, stands at a golden circular podium displaying the 'NAKHEEL PRESENTS DUBAI GLOBESOCCER AWARDS' logo. Speaking into a lapel microphone with a clear male British accent, he thanks Manchester City before transitioning to the next award, highlighting the presence of "world champions and continental champions" and "global superstars right here in the Middle East." The scene then cuts to Amanda Davies, a woman in a strapless black gown with subtle sparkles and a diamond necklace, also at the golden podium with a bottle of water beside her. With a clear female British accent, she adds that a "plethora of homegrown talent" makes "football in the Middle East... at its most formidable," evidenced by "brilliant performances over the last year." The camera returns to Tom Urquhart, who, with arms crossed then gesturing, asks, "who has been the player who delivered the very best and most consistently?" He then announces the award is "kindly sponsored by our friends at Italpreziosi" and prepares to reveal the finalists, all set to a consistent, upbeat orchestral background music. A graphic then transitions onto the screen, announcing the 'BEST MIDDLE EAST PLAYER' award with the 'ITALPREZIOSI' logo. Tom Urquhart's voice declares, "And the finalists then are..." as the nominees are displayed one by one in vertical frames against a glowing backdrop: Cristiano Ronaldo in a yellow Al Nassr jersey, Riyad Mahrez in a dark green Al-Ahli jersey, and Mohamed Elshenawy in a red Al Ahly jersey, each accompanied by applause as their names are spoken.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a golden animated graphic announcing the 'BEST MIDDLE EAST PLAYER' award, flanked by 'ITALPREZIOSI' logos, as a male voice announces "Riyad Mahrez" and then "Mohamed Al Shenawy," while Mohamed Elshenawy is shown in a red Al Ahly jersey. The scene transitions to a wide shot of the formally dressed audience, seated in white armchairs, some capturing the event on their phones. Amanda Davies, in a sparkling black gown and diamond necklace, stands at the golden circular podium with the 'NAKHEEL PRESENTS DUBAI GLOBESOCCER AWARDS' logo behind her, speaking into a microphone. She addresses the audience, announcing, "Ladies and gentlemen, the winner of the Dubai Globe Soccer Awards Best Middle East Player..." and builds suspense, stating, "I think the roof is about to go off. It's none other than... Cristiano Ronaldo!" This dramatic reveal is met with immediate, enthusiastic audience applause and cheers, accompanied by swelling music. Cristiano Ronaldo is seen in the audience, in a navy blue suit, attentively watching the stage, while the woman beside him smiles. The camera cuts back to Amanda Davies, with a large screen behind her displaying 'CRISTIANO RONALDO,' as she continues to praise his "magical year," mentioning his "54 goals in 2023" and that he "also secures you the Maradona Award, brought to you by Nakheel." She then highlights an "extraordinary" detail: "this trophy actually has the exact replica of the late great Diego Maradona's foot," which elicits further audience applause, as the scene alternates between her at the podium and the engaged audience.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a dynamic montage celebrating Cristiano Ronaldo's prolific scoring record, as a male narrator details his career, stating he is "further adding to his legacy by taking his talent to Al Nassr in Saudi Arabia" and that his "foresight made the globalization of football a reality." Text overlays like 'SCORED 54 GOALS IN 59 GAMES' and '44 GOALS FOR AL NASSR, 10 GOALS FOR PORTUGAL' flash over fast-paced clips of Ronaldo scoring and celebrating goals in his yellow and blue Al Nassr jersey, with close-ups and wide shots enhanced by sparks and fiery effects. The narrator emphasizes Ronaldo's "prowess on the pitch" and his "ability to make revolutionary decisions off it," concluding that "2023 ended with CR7 netting a staggering, world-leading 54 goals" for both Al Nassr and the Portuguese national team. The visuals then transition to a nighttime aerial view of the Atlantis The Palm Hotel, illuminated with blue and pink lights, establishing the Dubai setting. The scene shifts to the Dubai Globe Soccer Awards ceremony, where Ronaldo, dressed in a navy blue suit and black turtleneck, walks confidently down the stage aisle toward the podium. The grand stage features a dark backdrop with glowing orange and yellow geometric patterns, the 'NAKHEEL PRESENTS DUBAI GLOBESOCCER AWARDS' logo, and large screens displaying Ronaldo's image. He receives the 'Best Middle East Player of the Year and Maradona Award' from a man in a green thobe and white keffiyeh, shaking hands and posing for photos with the golden globe-shaped trophy. The audience, seated in white armchairs, applauds as Ronaldo walks back to his seat, while a male voice exclaims, "Another Globe Soccer Award for Cristiano!" and a female voice adds, "Congratulations, Cristiano!"

segments.integrated_caption: The video segment opens with Amanda Davies, dressed in a strapless black gown with subtle sparkles and a diamond necklace, holding a microphone and a blue folder, engaging Cristiano Ronaldo on stage. Her clear voice is heard asking Ronaldo about the importance of his first goal in Saudi Arabia, referencing past discussions about using criticism as motivation. Ronaldo, in a navy blue suit over a black turtleneck, stands beside a golden globe-shaped trophy on a black pedestal, with dynamic orange and yellow geometric light patterns animating the stage backdrop. As the camera zooms in, Ronaldo takes the microphone, his composed and reflective expression occasionally breaking into a warm smile. His voice, with a slight accent, confirms he remembers the goal, calling it a "good moment" and his "first one," expressing immense pride in his "amazing season." With a diamond earring glinting and neatly styled hair, he extends gratitude to his teammates and coaches from both Al Nassr and the Portugal National Team. Ronaldo continues, expressing pride in the golden trophy, which he identifies as the "best Middle East player" award, noting he'd never won it before and plans to display it passionately in his museum. He concludes by thanking his wife and kids for dealing with him, all while the vibrant stage lights continue to pulse in the background, maintaining the glamorous and formal ambiance.

The stage backdrop features dynamic, pulsing orange and yellow geometric light patterns, creating a celebratory atmosphere. His expression is composed and reflective, occasionally breaking into a warm smile as he addresses the audience. The lighting highlights his polished appearance, including a diamond earring and neatly styled hair. The on-screen text 'SILVER' and 'NORTH' briefly appears, possibly indicating a sponsor or segment title, while the overall ambiance remains formal and glamorous, consistent with the high-profile nature of the event.

segments.integrated_caption: The scene opens with a wide shot of the formally dressed audience at the Dubai Globe Soccer Awards, seated in white armchairs, with some attendees, including a man in a traditional white keffiyeh and a woman in a white lace dress, capturing the event on their phones. The visual then transitions to the stage where Amanda Davies, in a strapless black gown and diamond necklace, holds a microphone and a blue folder, engaging in conversation with Cristiano Ronaldo, who is dressed in a navy blue suit over a black turtleneck. Ronaldo's composed and reflective voice is heard, stating, "Sometimes I know I'm a little bit boring, but it's it's my job, you know, to be consistent every time. So, thank you to the Global Soccer for this amazing award." The stage backdrop features glowing orange and yellow geometric light patterns, with the 'NAKHEEL PRESENTS DUBAI GLOBESOCCER AWARDS' logo faintly visible. Amanda Davies then asks, "And you have said, you know, this is about more than just you. This is about growing football in the game in another region. How confident are you that the influx of players will continue?" The camera zooms in on Ronaldo, his diamond earring visible, as he responds, "I hope so. As I say before, the league... start one year ago to grow and grow. And I'm sure 100% they will continue to grow because our our boss, they decide and they love football. And in my opinion, the league will be top top top." The scene widens to show the full stage with the 'esaaD' logo illuminated behind them. Amanda Davies then exclaims, "Wow, top top top. You've got another trophy to collect if I can just usher you back over there. This is the pretty special Maradona award complete with a replica of the foot." A man in a green thobe and white keffiyeh then presents Ronaldo with a golden trophy, which Ronaldo accepts with a smile as the stage lights pulse rhythmically, creating a celebratory atmosphere.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a wide shot of the Dubai Globe Soccer Awards audience, including a man in a traditional white keffiyeh and a woman in a white lace dress, all seated in white armchairs and clapping enthusiastically. A female voice, identified as Amanda Davies, congratulates someone, followed by the sound of sustained audience applause and celebratory music. The scene transitions to a stage illuminated with dynamic orange and yellow geometric light patterns, featuring the 'NAKHEEL PRESENTS DUBAI GLOBESOCCER AWARDS' logo and sponsor logos. A male voice, belonging to the Host in Black Tuxedo, then congratulates Cristiano Ronaldo, noting he'll need "all his hands there for the two trophies" as he exits the stage. The Host continues, describing the qualities of a "tactical genius" and announcing that the Dubai Globe Soccer Awards will present an "esteemed list of best coach nominees." Amanda Davies then takes over, her voice declaring, "Your finalists for the Best Coach of the Year are..." as dramatic music builds. The video cuts to a graphic sequence for the 'BEST COACH' award, displaying nominees Marcel Koller, Josep Guardiola, and Carlo Ancelotti in vertical frames with their respective club crests against a dark background with shimmering golden light effects and floating orbs. Amanda Davies' voice announces "Marcel Koller" as the first nominee, accompanied by renewed audience applause and continuing music.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a dynamic animated sequence for the 'BEST COACH' award, featuring glowing orange and yellow geometric light patterns and floating orbs against a dark background, accompanied by upbeat, celebratory music. As the music plays, nominees are introduced in vertical frames: Carlo Ancelotti, in a grey suit with the Real Madrid crest, is announced by name, followed by Mikel Arteta in a black jacket with the Arsenal crest, also announced by name. Lionel Scaloni, in a blue Argentina national team polo with the Argentina crest, is then named, and finally, Simone Inzaghi, in a white shirt and black tie with the Inter Milan crest, is introduced. The scene transitions to a formally dressed audience seated in white armchairs, some clapping and others observing attentively. Tom Urquhart, a man in a black tuxedo with a bow tie, stands at a golden circular podium bearing the 'NAKHEEL PRESENTS DUBAI GLOBESOCCER AWARDS' logo, speaking into a lapel microphone. He announces, with a clear, formal tone, "Okay then, the winner... and the Best Coach of the Globe Soccer Awards is..." The camera returns to the audience, showing close-ups of individuals clapping and reacting enthusiastically as Urquhart declares, "Pep Guardiola!" This is met with widespread applause and cheering. The video then cuts to a montage of Josép Guardiola, initially in a white shirt with the Manchester City crest, as a narrator's voice begins, describing his impact: "With fundamental surrounding possession, pressing, creativity, Pep Guardiola has been at the forefront of the beautiful game's evolution." The montage continues with celebratory footage of Guardiola lifting trophies and being hoisted by his team, featuring dynamic camera angles and fiery visual effects, while on-screen text highlights his achievements: 'FIRST MANAGER TO WIN 4 FIFA CLUB WORLD CUP FINALS' and 'SECOND COACH TO WIN A HAT-TRICK OF PREMIER LEAGUE TITLES'. The narrator concludes, "Having stamped his authority in Barcelona and Munich, Guardiola's status was further elevated in Manchester City."

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a vibrant celebration of Manchester City's Premier League triumph, showing players in light blue jerseys joyfully lifting the Premier League trophy amidst a shower of confetti under a stadium arch, set to triumphant, energetic music and crowd cheering. The scene transitions to a sepia-toned montage of the team celebrating the UEFA Champions League victory, with the trophy held high and confetti falling, emphasizing their historic treble. A narrator's voice states, "After dominating the Premier League, he secured European then global success in 2023. The Champions League in June, followed by the Club World Cup triumph." On-screen text highlights Pep Guardiola as the 'FIRST MANAGER TO WIN A TREBLE TWICE' with Barcelona (2008–2009) and Manchester City (2022–2023). The narrator continues, "This is a coach who has an obsessive eye for detail, with the ability to get the very best out of every player he's worked with. 2023 was a special year for a special coach." The narrative then shifts to a close-up of Guardiola’s emotional face, followed by a dramatic shot of him holding the UEFA Champions League trophy against the illuminated backdrop of Atlantis The Palm Hotel in Dubai, with the text 'BEST COACH PEP GUARDIOLA' displayed, all accompanied by the continuing triumphant music. The scene transitions to the Dubai Globe Soccer Awards ceremony, where Guardiola, dressed in a black suit, walks onto the stage past an audibly applauding audience. He receives the 'Best Coach of the Year' award, a golden globe-shaped trophy, from a man in a green thobe and white keffiyeh, shaking hands and posing for photos. The stage is adorned with glowing orange and yellow geometric patterns, and the backdrop features the 'NAKHEEL PRESENTS DUBAI GLOBESOCCER AWARDS' logo. A woman, Amanda Davies, in a black strapless gown, congratulates him, saying, "Congratulations. Let me just bring you back this way a little bit," as applause swells. Guardiola then walks off stage with Amanda, who places a hand on his shoulder as they exit, the golden trophy in his hand reflecting the stage lights, with Amanda adding, "Thank you."

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a close-up of Amanda Davies, elegantly dressed in a sparkling black gown and diamond necklace, standing beside Pep Guardiola, who is sharp in a black suit and white shirt, holding a large, golden, globe-shaped trophy. Davies, holding a black microphone, places a hand on Guardiola’s shoulder as they walk off stage, smiling amidst the dynamic, pulsing orange and yellow geometric light patterns of the stage backdrop, which displays sponsor logos like 'ITALPREZIOSI' and 'ATLANTIS THE PALM DUBAI'. Davies's clear, articulate voice is heard congratulating Guardiola, "Firstly, happy birthday. Not a bad birthday celebration, getting your hands on another trophy. But what does it feel like being the best in the world?" Guardiola, speaking with a calm, reflective tone, responds, "Because we won. If we don't win, we are not even invited, so that is the truth. To win, there are many, many reasons, especially all of them are sitting there. And of course, we are here in UAE, where I would have been helped unconditionally. Yeah, really, really happy." The camera briefly cuts to the formally dressed audience, including Erling Haaland in a striking blue tuxedo, observing with a relaxed posture. Returning to the stage, Davies continues her interview, asking, "How much do you allow yourself to celebrate the successes?" Guardiola, gesturing with his free hand while holding the trophy, replies, "A lot. But you know, it's on to next as quick as possible, but of course we celebrate it. And especially when the days come through, the years come through, you'll remember. They will be not forgotten for all of us, that's for sure." The stage backdrop now shows 'SERIE A MADE IN ITALY' and 'MBA' logos. Davies then asks, "And have you managed to come up with an answer yet, which is the best, your Barcelona team or this one?" Guardiola chuckles slightly, concluding, "It's like us mom and dad to choose." The atmosphere remains warm and celebratory, acknowledging Guardiola's 'Best Coach of the Year' award.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with Amanda Davies, in a strapless black gown and diamond necklace, interviewing Pep Guardiola, who holds a golden globe-shaped trophy, on a stage illuminated by dynamic orange and yellow geometric light patterns and sponsor logos. Amanda playfully asks, "Which is where I'm a daddy's girl, personally," then laughs and congratulates Pep, telling him to "Go and join the team." As celebratory music and applause begin, the camera cuts to the formally dressed audience, including men in traditional white keffiyehs and women in elegant gowns, seated in white armchairs, applauding. A wide shot reveals the grand scale of the venue. Tom Urquhart, in a black tuxedo with a bow tie, then stands at a golden circular podium with the 'NAKHEEL PRESENTS DUBAI GLOBESOCCER AWARDS' logo, speaking into a lapel microphone. He congratulates Pep and the Man City team, then announces the next 'Career Award,' generously sponsored by Audi Al Nabooda Automobiles. Tom describes the recipient as someone who "really does epitomize inspiration, drive, and that all-important never-say-die attitude," building suspense before exclaiming, "Casemiro!" This announcement is met with loud applause and cheering from the audience, including Kyle Walker and a woman in a sparkling white gown. The segment concludes with a dynamic graphic featuring a close-up of a footballer's face with the text '3 X FIFA CLUB WORLD CUP WINNER' in bold orange and white letters, accompanied by fiery visual effects and a glowing soccer ball animation.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a dynamic montage of football highlights, showcasing Casemiro in a white jersey scoring goals and celebrating, set to an upbeat, dramatic musical score. A male narrator describes Casemiro as a player of "speed, strength, and power," "one of the world's leading central defensive midfielders" whose "physical attributes combine seamlessly with guile and goal scoring." Text overlays announce his '2019 COPA AMERICA CHAMPION' and '5 X UEFA CHAMPIONS LEAGUE WINNER' titles, while the narrator highlights his "astonishing 25 trophies" from an "illustrious career" with "Sao Paulo, Real Madrid, and now Manchester United." The scene transitions to a grand, illuminated view of Atlantis The Palm Hotel at dusk, with fireworks and a golden soccer ball animation, as the music shifts to a celebratory, electronic track. This leads to the reveal of 'PLAYER CAREER AWARD CASEMIRO' in bold text. The setting then shifts to the Dubai Globe Soccer Awards ceremony, where Casemiro, dressed in a black tuxedo with a bow tie, walks confidently down a curved, illuminated stage toward the podium. He is joined by a man in a green thobe and white keffiyeh, who presents him with a golden, globe-shaped trophy. The stage backdrop features glowing orange and yellow geometric patterns and sponsor logos. The audience, seated at white-clothed tables, applauds as Casemiro accepts the award, smiling and posing for photos with the presenter, just as a female voice begins to announce, "Congratulations and now..."

audio_updated_entities.Casemiro: A man in a black tuxedo with a bow tie, walking confidently down the stage, receiving the 'Player Career Award' with a smile, and posing with the golden globe-shaped trophy. He is extensively described by a male narrator as a player with "speed, strength, and power," "one of the world's leading central defensive midfielders," whose "physical attributes combine seamlessly with guile and goal scoring." He "thrives in the big moments" and has "won an astonishing 25 trophies" throughout his "illustrious career" with "Sao Paulo, Real Madrid, and now Manchester United."

segments.integrated_caption: The Dubai Globe Soccer Awards stage opens with a wide shot, illuminated by dynamic orange and yellow geometric light patterns, prominently displaying the 'NAKHEEL PRESENTS DUBAI GLOBESOCCER AWARDS' logo. The formally dressed audience, including many in traditional white keffiyehs, is seated attentively. The focus shifts to Casemiro, clad in a black tuxedo with a bow tie, standing at a golden circular 'NAKHEEL' branded podium. He adjusts the microphone and places a bottle of water beside him, as a female voice introduces him to speak a few words in Portuguese. Behind him, a large screen displays his name 'CASEMIRO' in bold white letters alongside a red-clad footballer image, while the stage backdrop pulses with light effects and sponsor logos. Casemiro, speaking calmly and confidently in Portuguese, begins by greeting everyone and expressing his gratitude to Dubai and Globe Soccer for the "great event." He states it's a pleasure to receive such an award, calling it a "great detail" in his career. He proceeds to thank his former clubs – São Paulo, Futebol Clube do Porto, Real Madrid Castilla, Real Madrid, and Manchester – acknowledging that without them, he wouldn't have achieved his titles or become the person he is today. As he speaks, occasionally glancing at notes and using expressive hand gestures, the camera cuts to a side view showing two men in traditional white thobes and keffiyehs respectfully observing beside the podium. The warm, celebratory lighting emphasizes the prestigious atmosphere. Casemiro concludes by offering special thanks to his family and his wife, Ana Mariana, who has always accompanied him, while the golden 'Player Career Award' trophy rests nearby on a pedestal.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a close-up of a woman in a sparkling white gown smiling in the audience, before cutting to Casemiro, a man in a black tuxedo, speaking expressively in Portuguese from a golden podium, his name 'CASEMIRO' displayed on the vibrant, dynamically lit stage backdrop. He expresses deep gratitude, concluding with "muito obrigado a todos" (thank you very much to everyone), which is met with enthusiastic applause from the formally dressed audience, including those in traditional white keffiyehs. The scene transitions to a wide shot of the glamorous Dubai Globe Soccer Awards stage, adorned with sponsor logos and pulsing orange and yellow lights. Amanda Davies, in a black strapless gown, then takes the golden podium, speaking clearly in English into a microphone while holding a tablet. She translates Casemiro's sentiments, noting his gratitude for the recognition, his thanks to all the clubs he's represented, and his dedication of the award to his wife, Ana, who is present in the audience. Amanda also highlights Casemiro's youth at 31 and his ambition to win "a whole lot more." The camera alternates between Amanda and the attentive audience, where the woman in the white gown is seen receiving a golden globe-shaped trophy. Finally, Tom Urquhart, also in a black tuxedo, takes the podium, delivering a speech with animated gestures into his lapel microphone. He introduces the next segment, acknowledging clubs that have "redefined the game of football," whether "on the pitch or of course in the boardroom," emphasizing their "consistent" climb towards the pinnacle, maintaining the celebratory and prestigious atmosphere of the event.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with Tom Urquhart, a man in a black tuxedo with a bow tie, standing at a golden circular podium emblazoned with the 'NAKHEEL PRESENTS DUBAI GLOBESOCCER AWARDS' logo. He speaks into a lapel microphone, gesturing with his hands, explaining how the "accomplishment of goals" and becoming a "global football force" wouldn't be possible "without the direction from the club president," as dynamic orange and yellow geometric light patterns pulse behind him. The scene transitions to Amanda Davies, in a strapless black gown with subtle sparkles and a diamond necklace, who takes the podium. She speaks with a composed expression, her blonde hair in an updo, noting that "Over the last year, there have been clubs who have taken their place in the upper echelons of the football world" and consolidated their positions "as global leaders all through the vision and efforts of the president." She then announces, "The finalists then for the best president at this, the Nakheel Dubai Globe Soccer Awards. And the finalists are:" The camera cuts back to Tom Urquhart, who continues the presentation, announcing the nominees. The segment then shifts to a graphic sequence for the 'BEST PRESIDENT' award, displaying the nominees as Tom Urquhart names them: "Manchester City's Khaldoon Al Mubarak," showing him in a blue suit with the Manchester City crest; "Joan Laporta of FC Barcelona," in a black tuxedo with the Barcelona crest; and "Sevilla's José Castro," in a dark suit with a red tie, holding a football, with the Sevilla crest behind him. Throughout the announcements, the audience, seated in white armchairs, is shown clapping and attentively watching the stage, with applause audible. The video returns to Amanda Davies, who smiles and continues her speech, beginning to announce "The winner...", maintaining the formal and celebratory atmosphere of the Dubai Globe Soccer Awards, with background music and applause continuing.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with Amanda Davies, elegantly dressed in a strapless black gown and diamond necklace, standing at a golden circular podium against a dynamic backdrop of pulsing orange and yellow geometric light patterns, her voice clear as she announces the 'Dubai Globesoccer Awards Best President.' Following her announcement, the camera cuts to the audience where Erling Haaland, in a striking blue tuxedo, claps enthusiastically. Amanda then declares, "Khaldoon Al Mubarak of Manchester City," as an upbeat, celebratory instrumental track begins to play. On stage, three men in traditional Emirati attire, including Khaldoon Al Mubarak in a white thobe, walk toward the podium. Khaldoon receives a large, golden, globe-shaped trophy from the Presenter in Dark Suit, who stands at the second podium. On-screen text confirms the award as 'Best President' for Khaldoon Al Mubarak. Amanda's voice continues, praising Khaldoon's leadership, stating, "City's five trophies is a testament to their growth under the leadership of Khaldoon Al Mubarak." She highlights Manchester City's journey and their president providing the blueprint for their "seismic influence on the beautiful game." The Presenter in Dark Suit then takes over, his voice resonating as he adds, "Yeah, on the pitch, they continue to reach new heights, off it, they've just been as successful," mentioning the club's Premier League record revenue for the 22-23 financial year and the City Football Group's expanding network, concluding that "a lasting legacy has been created." He describes it as a "15-year journey" to the "summit of world football," before inviting Khaldoon to speak. Throughout, Khaldoon smiles proudly, holding the trophy alongside the Recipient in Green Thobe and the Recipient in White Thobe, while the formally dressed audience watches attentively, some clapping, all set against the vibrant stage backdrop featuring the Dubai Globesoccer Awards logo and sponsor logos.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video opens with Amanda Davies, in a strapless black gown and diamond necklace, standing at a golden circular podium with the 'NAKHEEL PRESENTS DUBAI GLOBESOCCER AWARDS' logo, speaking into a microphone against a dynamic backdrop of pulsing orange and yellow geometric light patterns. The scene cuts to the audience, where Erling Haaland, in a striking blue tuxedo with a white bow tie, is seen clapping enthusiastically. The camera then shifts to the stage, where three men in traditional Emirati attire—Khaldoon Al Mubarak in a white thobe, a man in a dark green thobe, and another in a light grey thobe—walk toward the podium. Khaldoon Al Mubarak receives a large, golden, globe-shaped trophy from the Presenter in Dark Suit, who is standing at the second podium. On-screen text identifies the award as 'Best President' and names Khaldoon Al Mubarak. The three men stand together on stage, with Khaldoon holding the trophy, smiling and looking around. The stage backdrop features the Dubai Globesoccer Awards logo, sponsor logos including 'NAKHEEL' and 'DUBAI SPORTS COUNCIL', and large screens displaying Khaldoon's image and name. The audience, dressed in formal attire, watches attentively, with some clapping. The scene concludes with a wide shot of the stage, showing the three recipients, the two podiums, and the full audience, emphasizing the grandeur of the event.

segments.integrated_caption: The Dubai Globe Soccer Awards stage is brightly lit with dynamic orange and yellow geometric patterns, showcasing the 'NAKHEEL PRESENTS DUBAI GLOBESOCCER AWARDS' title and sponsor logos. Amanda Davies, elegant in a sparkling strapless black gown and diamond necklace, holds a microphone, her clear, professional voice heard offering "So many congratulations" before asking Khaldoon Al Mubarak, who stands beside her in a traditional white thobe and keffiyeh, about Manchester City's sustained dominance: "how long can Manchester City keep this going?" A large golden trophy rests on a 'NAKHEEL' pedestal nearby. Khaldoon, identified as 'Best President' on screen, responds with a calm, measured voice, expressing gratitude to "His Highness Sheikh Mansour bin Mohammed bin Rashid" and "all the organizers" for the award. He humbly states his job isn't "that hard" when you have "the best players in the world," "the best coaching staff in the world," "the best organization in the world," "the best football director in Chiki Begiristan in the world," and "the best CEO, Ferran Soriano, in the world." As he speaks with expressive hand movements, the camera briefly cuts to the attentive, formally dressed audience, including Erling Haaland in a striking blue tuxedo, before returning to Khaldoon's composed and respectful delivery on stage, the grandeur of the event enhanced by the celebratory lighting.

A large golden trophy rests on a black pedestal labeled 'NAKHEEL' beside them. Amanda holds a microphone and engages Khaldoon in conversation, gesturing with her free hand while he responds with expressive hand movements, occasionally clasping his hands. segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with Amanda Davies, elegant in a sparkling black gown and diamond necklace, interviewing a man in traditional white Emirati attire on stage. A large golden globe trophy on a 'NAKHEEL' pedestal stands to their left, while the stage backdrop pulses with dynamic orange and yellow geometric lights and sponsor logos like 'NAKHEEL' and 'CNN'. The man, speaking into Amanda's microphone, articulately praises Manchester City's success, crediting "the best owner in his Highness Sheikh Mansour" for unwavering support and "the best manager in the world in Pep Guardiola," adding that with "the best fans in the world," his job is "pretty easy." The camera cuts to the formally dressed audience, some in traditional keffiyehs, listening intently and then applauding audibly. Returning to the stage, Amanda asks, "How long can you keep him, Pat?", to which the man confidently replies that continuous winning comes from "a commitment for excellence," as the audience's applause continues, enhancing the celebratory atmosphere of the Nakheel Dubai Globe Soccer Awards.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video segment opens with Amanda Davies, dressed in an elegant strapless black gown adorned with subtle sparkles and a diamond necklace, conducting an interview on stage with a man in traditional white Emirati attire (thobe and ghutra). She holds a black microphone with a silver grille and blue accent ring, extending it toward the man, who gestures expressively with his hands as he speaks. segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with Tom Urquhart, a man in a black tuxedo with a bow tie, standing at a golden circular podium branded 'NAKHEEL PRESENTS DUBAI GLOBESOCCER AWARDS'. He speaks into a lapel microphone, gesturing expressively, discussing how "every single professional athlete" started as a "dreamer," seeking "recognition from their most loyal fans" and finding "no greater satisfaction" than "bringing smiles to the faces of fans." Dynamic orange and yellow geometric light patterns pulse in the background. Amanda Davies, in a sparkling strapless black gown and diamond necklace, then takes over at the same podium, speaking with a confident smile. She continues, stating that "fans from around the world, quite literally, have cast their vote in the hope of seeing their favourite footballer being crowned the Fan's Favourite Player of the Year." She then announces, "Let's have a look at their finalists then, and the finalists are..." The video cuts to a graphic announcing the 'FAN'S FAVOURITE PLAYER OF THE YEAR' award, featuring nominees Cristiano Ronaldo in a yellow Al Nassr jersey, Lionel Messi in a pink Inter Miami jersey, and Mohamed Salah in a red Liverpool jersey. As each player's image appears, Amanda announces their name, met with enthusiastic applause from the formally dressed audience, which includes men in traditional white keffiyehs. The segment concludes with Amanda Davies returning to the podium, smiling and continuing her presentation, with the stage lighting and branding remaining prominent throughout, as she begins to announce the winner.

The scene cuts to the formally attired audience, including men in traditional white keffiyehs and women in gowns, who are seated in white armchairs, clapping and cheering enthusiastically in response to the announcement. An upbeat, dramatic musical score then accompanies a high-energy montage celebrating Cristiano Ronaldo’s career. The montage begins with a close-up of him in a yellow Al Nassr jersey, followed by rapid-fire clips of him scoring goals in various iconic kits—Manchester United red, Real Madrid white, Juventus black and white, and Al Nassr yellow. On-screen text highlights his achievements: '873 GOALS FOR CLUB AND COUNTRY', 'TOP GOAL SCORER IN INTERNATIONAL FOOTBALL – 128 GOALS', and 'MOST CAPED INTERNATIONAL FOOTBALLER – 205 APPEARANCES'. The voiceover continues, noting, "His historic move to Al Nassr marked a significant step in the evolution of Middle Eastern football, and his continued excellence on the field of play is tribute to his unwavering dedication to his craft." The montage includes dramatic goal celebrations, powerful shots, and close-ups of Ronaldo’s intense expressions, all overlaid with fiery particle effects and dynamic transitions. The voiceover concludes, "Ronaldo has a unique ability to captivate fans across the globe, drawing votes from every corner of the world. segments.integrated_caption: Cristiano Ronaldo, sharply dressed in a navy blue suit and black turtleneck, stands at a golden circular podium, delivering an acceptance speech into two microphones. He speaks passionately, with a slight accent, about the importance of fans, recalling the challenges of playing in empty stadiums during COVID, stating, "without you guys, the football is nothing." He expresses gratitude for being voted for, prompting loud applause and cheers from the formally dressed audience, many of whom are recording on their phones. Ronaldo then encourages continued support for football, Al Nassr, and himself, eliciting further cheers. As upbeat, celebratory music begins to play, a female voice announces, "Congratulations, Cristiano Ronaldo, ladies and gentlemen." The camera pulls back to reveal the grand stage setup, featuring multiple podiums, sponsor logos like 'NAKHEEL' and 'AUDI', and a central screen displaying 'DUBAI GLOBESOCCER AWARDS'. Two other figures, one in a green thobe and another in a white thobe, stand beside him. The scene then shifts to Tom Urquhart, in a black tuxedo, speaking into a lapel microphone at a similar golden podium, his clear, formal British accent introducing, "And so, my friends, it comes down to this, the grand finale, the final..." against the dynamic orange and yellow light patterns of the stage.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with Tom Urquhart, dressed in a black tuxedo with a bow tie, standing at a golden circular podium bearing the 'NAKHEEL PRESENTS DUBAI GLOBESOCCER AWARDS' logo. He speaks into a lapel microphone, gesturing with his hands, thanking the "millions that have tuned in around the world" and describing the past year in football as "from the sublime to the magical" and "downright astonishing," highlighting "defensive resilience, midfield mastery, goal scoring supremacy," all against a backdrop of pulsing orange and yellow geometric light patterns and accompanied by triumphant orchestral music. After a few moments, Amanda Davies, wearing a sparkling strapless black gown and diamond necklace, appears at the same podium, smiling warmly. She announces, "So, it is time to crown the best of the best. Your nominees are..." as the music swells. The video then cuts to a golden animated graphic for the 'BEST MEN'S PLAYER' award, featuring shimmering light trails and floating orbs. Amanda Davies then announces each nominee as they appear in vertical frames against the dynamic golden background: "Cristiano Ronaldo!" in a yellow Al Nassr jersey, followed by "Erling Haaland!" in a blue Manchester City jersey, and finally "Mohamed Salah!" in a red Liverpool jersey, each announcement met with crowd applause and cheers.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with an energetic, dramatic musical score accompanying a dynamic animated sequence for the 'BEST MEN'S PLAYER' award. Vertical frames against a dark, shimmering golden background showcase nominees, with a female voice distinctly announcing each name: first, Jude Bellingham in his Real Madrid jersey, followed by Bernardo Silva in a Manchester City jersey, then Lionel Messi in an Inter Miami jersey, Karim Benzema in an Al Ittihad jersey, Kylian Mbappé in a Paris Saint-Germain jersey, Victor Osimhen in a Napoli jersey, and finally, Vinícius Jr. in a Real Madrid jersey. Each player's name and club crest are visible beneath their image. The scene then transitions to a wide shot of the formally dressed audience, including some in traditional Emirati attire, seated in white armchairs. The camera cuts to a split-screen, showing Cristiano Ronaldo in a dark blue suit and Erling Haaland in a bright blue suit, both seated with serious, observant expressions. Tom Urquhart, the host, is then seen at a golden circular podium with the 'NAKHEEL PRESENTS DUBAI GLOBESOCCER AWARDS' logo, speaking into a lapel microphone against a backdrop of pulsing orange and yellow geometric light patterns. His voice is heard, remarking, "It is quite the list, is it not? But one of those names has been crowned the final winner of the evening." He continues, addressing the "ladies, gentlemen, honored guests," as the split-screen of Ronaldo and Haaland returns, capturing their reactions. Urquhart then announces, "Best Men's Player of the Year at this year's Nakheel Dubai Globe Soccer Award goes to..." building anticipation as the music swells and audience applause begins.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a split-screen view of the audience at the Nakheel Dubai Globe Soccer Awards, showing Cristiano Ronaldo in a dark blue suit, resting his chin on his hand, and Erling Haaland in a bright blue suit with a white bow tie, sitting upright. Upbeat, celebratory music plays as a male voice shouts "Haaland!" then "Erling Haaland!", followed by applause, coinciding with a close-up of Haaland clapping. The scene transitions to a dynamic montage of Haaland's football highlights as a narrator begins, "With a combination of power, pace, and intricate movement," explaining that "Erling Haaland has scored against some of the world's best defenses." The narrator continues, stating that "Under the tutelage of Pep Guardiola, Haaland scored a Premier League record 36 goals in 35 league appearances in his debut season," while on-screen text confirms him as the 'TOP SCORER IN THE PREMIER LEAGUE WITH 36 GOALS IN 35 APPEARANCES,' accompanied by footage of him celebrating and scoring goals. The montage continues with on-screen text announcing him as 'TOP SCORER IN THE CHAMPIONS LEAGUE WITH 12 GOALS IN 11 APPEARANCES,' showcasing powerful strikes and goal celebrations, as the narrator adds that he "also finishes as the top scorer in City's triumphant run to the Champions League glory with 12 goals," describing him as a "once-in-a-generation goalscorer" seamlessly fitting into a team of creative talents. A rapid sequence of goals is shown, culminating in the text '50 GOALS IN 2023,' emphasizing his prolific scoring, with the narrator concluding that these "50 goals in 2023 helped City win five trophies in the year." The video ends with Haaland raising his fist in triumph against a backdrop of Dubai's skyline, including Atlantis The Palm Hotel, accompanied by a "whoosh" sound effect before transitioning to a stage shot. On stage, with the music swelling and applause, a male announcer declares, "Men's Player of the Year, Erling Haaland!", as the award graphic 'MEN'S PLAYER OF THE YEAR' with his name in bold gold letters is displayed.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video opens with a split-screen view of the audience at the Nakheel Dubai Globe Soccer Awards, focusing on Cristiano Ronaldo in a dark blue suit, resting his chin on his hand, and Erling Haaland in a bright blue suit with a white bow tie, sitting upright with hands on the armrests. The scene transitions to a close-up of Haaland clapping, then shifts to a dynamic montage of his football highlights. On-screen text announces him as the 'TOP SCORER IN THE PREMIER LEAGUE WITH 36 GOALS IN 35 APPEARANCES,' followed by footage of him celebrating and scoring goals. The montage continues with 'TOP SCORER IN THE CHAMPIONS LEAGUE WITH 12 GOALS IN 11 APPEARANCES,' showcasing powerful strikes and goal celebrations. A rapid sequence of goals is shown, culminating in the text '50 GOALS IN 2023,' emphasizing his prolific scoring. The video ends with Haaland raising his fist in triumph against a backdrop of Dubai's skyline, including Atlantis The Palm Hotel, before transitioning to a stage shot where he is announced as 'MEN'S PLAYER OF THE YEAR' with his name displayed in bold gold letters.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with Erling Haaland, striking in a royal blue double-breasted suit with a white bow tie and his blonde hair slicked back in a bun, walking confidently down the aisle towards the stage at the Dubai Globe Soccer Awards. Upbeat, celebratory music plays as a male announcer's voice declares, "So the final award of the evening, the crowning glory, the best men's player of the year. This year's Dubai Globe Soccer Awards brought to you by Nakheel. Erling Haaland!" The grand hall is filled with formally dressed audience members, many in traditional Emirati attire, who erupt in loud applause and cheers as Haaland ascends the stage. The stage backdrop glows with dynamic orange and yellow geometric light patterns and displays event branding like 'NAKHEEL PRESENTS DUBAI GLOBESOCCER AWARDS' and sponsor logos including Audi and SUSHISAMBA DUBAI. He is greeted by two men in traditional Emirati thobes—one in green and one in white—who present him with a large, golden, globe-shaped trophy. On-screen text identifies the award: 'Erling Haaland Best Men's Player of the Year'. After a brief acknowledgment, Haaland steps aside as the camera pans to the still-applauding audience. He then joins Amanda Davies, the host in a strapless black gown with a diamond necklace, for an on-stage interview. She holds a microphone and a blue folder, asking Haaland, "Erling, congratulations. Just a couple of words if I may. It's almost easy to forget at times that it was only last season you joined City. What is it about this club, squad, this team that has made it so easy for you to settle in and shine?" Haaland, speaking into the mic with a distinct, slightly deep voice and Norwegian accent, gestures subtly with his hands as he replies, "Well, I think first, first of all, the people... the guys that sits here is, uh, is a bunch of amazing people. Uh, and then you have the, the manager, of course, who is, uh, is not so bad as well. Uh, so, uh, yeah, it's a, it's a fantastic club and, um," The stage lighting remains vibrant with pulsing orange and yellow effects, and sponsor logos are visible in the background, maintaining the celebratory and formal atmosphere of his recognition.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video opens with Erling Haaland, dressed in a striking royal blue double-breasted suit with a white bow tie and his blonde hair slicked back in a bun, walking confidently down the aisle toward the stage at the Dubai Globe Soccer Awards. The venue is a grand hall filled with formally dressed audience members, including many in traditional Emirati attire, seated at white-clothed tables. As Haaland ascends the stage, the backdrop glows with dynamic orange and yellow geometric light patterns and displays the event's branding, including 'NAKHEEL PRESENTS DUBAI GLOBESOCCER AWARDS' and sponsor logos like Audi and SUSHISAMBA DUBAI. He is greeted by two men in traditional Emirati thobes—one in green and one in white—who present him with a large, golden, globe-shaped trophy. On-screen text identifies the award: 'Erling Haaland Best Men's Player of the Year'. After a brief moment of acknowledgment, Haaland steps to the side as the camera pans to the audience, who are applauding. He then joins Amanda Davies, the host in a strapless black gown with a diamond necklace, for an on-stage interview. segments.integrated_caption: On stage at the Nakheel Presents Dubai Globe Soccer Awards, Amanda Davies, elegant in a strapless black gown with subtle sparkles and a diamond necklace, holds a microphone and gestures as she interviews Erling Haaland. Haaland, in a bright blue double-breasted suit with a white bow tie and his blonde hair in a bun, stands beside her, hands clasped, occasionally smiling and nodding. The stage backdrop features dynamic, pulsing orange and yellow geometric light patterns, with sponsor logos like 'KOORA BREAK' and 'Audi' visible. Haaland's distinct, accented voice is heard explaining, "...the support we players get from from the administration and and everything and the leaders in the club is is massive as well. So, uh, it's a nice club and uh and I really enjoy being here." Amanda, speaking in a clear, engaging voice, then asks, "Not a bad job at all. Have you still got the Champions League as your ringtone on your phone now you've won it and done it?" Haaland confirms, "Yeah, I do. Uh, and uh I uh I love this competition. Uh, and yeah, it was a was a big dream when when we won it. Uh, and uh fantastic feeling." Amanda follows up, "Do you know when we're going to see you back?" to which Haaland replies, "I hope really soon because I'm getting a bit sick of uh not playing. So, uh, hopefully soon." Amanda concludes, "Great. We wish you well with your recovery and congratulations once again." She then addresses the audience, "Ladies and gentlemen, Erling, oh! Oh, hang on a minute, I got a bit carried away. Before we let you go, we've got some special messages." During this exchange, the camera briefly cuts to a close-up of Marcel Koller in the audience, observing with a serious expression, while the focused lighting maintains the glamorous and celebratory atmosphere.

Amanda Davies gestures with her free hand while speaking, and Haaland maintains a composed, respectful demeanor throughout the exchange.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with Amanda Davies, in a sparkling black gown and diamond necklace, addressing Erling Haaland, who stands beside her on stage in a bright blue double-breasted suit, his blonde hair in a bun, against a dark backdrop with pulsing orange and yellow geometric lights. Amanda's clear, professional voice is heard saying, 'from some of your fans, just have a look,' as the scene transitions. Upbeat, celebratory music with a driving beat begins, accompanying a montage of young boys in blue and black striped 'DHL' football kits enthusiastically mimicking Haaland’s iconic meditation pose on sunlit pitches. These clips are interspersed with real match footage of Haaland scoring goals, including a powerful header and a strike, with intense football commentary like 'Erling Haaland finish!' and on-screen text highlighting his achievements. Throughout, a chorus of children's voices, speaking in various languages, excitedly shout 'Congrats Haaland!', 'Congratulations Haaland!', 'Mabrook Haaland!', 'Bravo Haaland!', and 'Congratulazioni Haaland!', expressing their admiration. The scene then shifts to a dusty field in Malawi, where a young boy in an orange bib and a group of boys perform the same meditation pose, echoing the global impact of Haaland's celebration. The sequence returns to the Dubai awards, with Haaland smiling as he walks off stage with Amanda Davies amidst the continued celebratory audio. The camera then cuts to the audience, where Pep Guardiola, in a black suit, is seen clapping with a thoughtful expression, while other formally dressed attendees, including men in traditional white keffiyehs, applaud, their claps distinctly audible. The final shot is a close-up of a young boy in a blue and black kit, smiling directly at the camera with the Dubai skyline visible, as Amanda's voice returns, asking, 'Amazing. What does that mean when you see that?'

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of Amanda Davies, elegant in a sparkling black gown and diamond necklace, interviewing Erling Haaland, who sports a bright blue double-breasted suit and a white bow tie, his blonde hair in a bun. Haaland, speaking with a calm, reflective voice, shares that his career shows "no matter where you come from... you can come far," emphasizing hard work and having the right people around you. Davies, holding a black microphone with a silver grille and blue accent ring, then congratulates him, saying "Amazing. Congratulations. Once more, ladies and gentlemen, Erling Haaland," as Haaland walks off stage to the sound of the audience clapping from their white armchairs. The scene transitions to a wide shot of the stage, displaying the 'NAKHEEL PRESENTS DUBAI GLOBESOCCER AWARDS' logo against a dark backdrop with pulsing orange and yellow geometric light patterns. Tom Urquhart, in a black tuxedo, stands at a golden circular podium, his clear, formal voice resonating as he addresses Haaland, "Yeah, Erling, congratulations to you," and describes the "magical setting of the Atlantis the Palm." Amanda Davies then appears at a second golden podium, holding a blue folder, her professional voice announcing, "the 14th edition of the Globe Soccer Awards has delivered a spectacle," inviting all winners to join Erling on stage for a "truly magical evening." Urquhart's voice returns, inviting the audience to "sit back," all set against dynamic golden light patterns and uplifting awards ceremony music.

segments.integrated_caption: Against a vibrant Stage Backdrop of pulsing orange and yellow geometric lights, Tom Urquhart, in a black tuxedo, speaks from the Golden Podium, encouraging applause for "the star performers of 2023." Amanda Davies, in a strapless black gown with a diamond necklace, joins him at the Second Podium, gesturing as she announces, "A huge congratulations to the simply incredible Cristiano Ronaldo, please come and join us," which is met with audience applause. Tom then states they will "welcome back onto stage the entire Manchester City team, backroom staff, all the winners," with Amanda adding, "Including their coach, coach of the year, Pep Guardiola," as the audience applauds again. A wide view of the grand Event Venue Interior shows formally dressed Audience Members in white armchairs, with the stage featuring two golden podiums and the Nakheel Dubai Globe Soccer Awards logo. Tom continues, inviting "career award winners as well, of course, John Terry," then "Lionel Scaloni," and "the Brazilian Manchester United great Casemiro" to join them on stage, followed by more applause. Dignitaries, including a Recipient in White Thobe and a Recipient in Green Thobe, flank the stage. Amanda expresses gratitude to "His Highness Sheikh Mansour bin Mohammed bin Rashid Al Maktoum, Chairman of the Dubai Sports Council, and His Excellency Mattar Al Tayer, Vice Chairman of the Dubai Sports Council for gracing us with their presence," drawing further applause. Men in suits, including Kyle Walker and Cristiano Ronaldo, are seen walking through audience aisles, some holding Golden Trophies. Close-ups show Marcel Koller engaged and Pep Guardiola clapping thoughtfully. The scene culminates with multiple award winners, including Erling Haaland, gathering on stage with their trophies alongside dignitaries and hosts, under the glowing Dubai Globe Soccer Awards logo, as Tom begins to call on more individuals amidst the celebratory atmosphere and dynamic lighting.
